name: env-vars
context: Vercel environment variables — development, preview, and production scopes, encrypted secrets
info: Vercel environment variables provide configuration and secrets to builds and serverless functions. Each variable is scoped to one or more environments: development (local via `vercel dev`), preview (branch and PR deployments), and production (production domain deployments). Variables can be plain text or encrypted (Sensitive). Encrypted variables are stored at rest with AES-256 and decrypted only at build time or function runtime. Use the dashboard, CLI (`vercel env`), or API to manage variables. Variables are injected as `process.env` in Node.js, `os.environ` in Python, and `os.Getenv()` in Go. For local development, `vercel env pull` writes a `.env.local` file. Never commit secrets to source control — use Vercel environment variables instead.
triggers: pull env vars for local development
body:
# Environment Variables

Vercel environment variables store configuration and secrets for builds and runtime. Each variable is scoped to specific environments, ensuring preview deployments use different values than production.

## Scopes

| Scope | When Used | Example |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| **Production** | Deployments on the production branch | `DATABASE_URL=postgres://prod-db/...` |
| **Preview** | Branch and PR deployments | `DATABASE_URL=postgres://staging-db/...` |
| **Development** | Local dev via `vercel dev` | `DATABASE_URL=postgres://localhost/...` |

A single variable can be assigned to multiple scopes. Common pattern: same key name, different values per scope.

## Managing Variables

### CLI

```bash
# Add a variable (interactive scope selection)
vercel env add DATABASE_URL

# Add with specific scope
vercel env add API_KEY production
vercel env add API_KEY preview

# List all variables
vercel env ls

# Remove a variable
vercel env rm DATABASE_URL production

# Pull to .env.local for local development
vercel env pull .env.local
```

### API

```bash
curl -X POST "https://api.vercel.com/v10/projects/$VERCEL_PROJECT_ID/env" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $VERCEL_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "key": "DATABASE_URL",
    "value": "postgres://prod-db/myapp",
    "target": ["production"],
    "type": "encrypted"
  }'
```

### Dashboard

Navigate to Project Settings > Environment Variables. Add variables with scope checkboxes and optional encryption.

## Encrypted (Sensitive) Variables

Mark variables as "Sensitive" to encrypt them at rest with AES-256. Encrypted variables:

- Are **not** visible in the dashboard after creation (write-only)
- Are decrypted only at build time and function runtime
- Cannot be read via the API after creation
- Appear as `***` in build logs

Use encryption for database credentials, API keys, private keys, and any secret that should not be exposed in logs or UI.

## Local Development

Pull variables to a local `.env.local` file:

```bash
vercel env pull .env.local
```

This writes development-scoped variables to `.env.local`. Add `.env.local` to `.gitignore` — never commit secrets. The `vercel dev` command automatically loads these variables.

## System Environment Variables

Vercel automatically injects system variables:

| Variable | Value |
|----------|--------|
| `VERCEL` | `1` (always set on Vercel) |
| `VERCEL_ENV` | `production`, `preview`, or `development` |
| `VERCEL_URL` | Deployment URL (without protocol) |
| `VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA` | Git commit SHA |
| `VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_MESSAGE` | Git commit message |
| `VERCEL_GIT_REPO_SLUG` | Repository name |
| `VERCEL_GIT_REPO_OWNER` | Repository owner |

sections.title: Environment scopes
sections.body: Each variable targets one or more scopes: `production` (live domain traffic via [[vercel/domains]]), `preview` (branch/PR deployments via [[vercel/deploy#preview]]), and `development` (local `vercel dev`). Different values per scope allow safe separation of production secrets from development credentials. Use `VERCEL_ENV` system variable to detect the current scope at runtime.

sections.title: Adding and managing variables
sections.body: Use `vercel env add <KEY> [scope]` to add via CLI. Use `vercel env ls` to list. Use the dashboard for visual management. The API supports programmatic CRUD operations. Variables are available in [[vercel/serverless]] functions via `process.env` and in [[vercel/edge]] functions via the edge runtime environment. Changes take effect on the next deployment — existing deployments are not affected.

sections.title: Encrypted secrets
sections.body: Mark variables as `type: encrypted` (API) or "Sensitive" (dashboard) for AES-256 encryption at rest. Encrypted values are write-only — they cannot be read back via API or dashboard after creation. They are decrypted at build time and injected into the runtime environment. Use for database URLs, API keys, signing secrets, and private keys. Encrypted variables appear as `***` in build logs.

sections.title: Local development
sections.body: Run `vercel env pull .env.local` to download development-scoped variables to a local file. The `.env.local` file is loaded by `vercel dev`, Next.js dev server, and other frameworks automatically. Always add `.env.local` to `.gitignore`. For team workflows, each developer pulls their own copy. Use `vercel dev` for full parity with the Vercel runtime including [[vercel/serverless]] function execution.

links.context: Environment variables are applied during deployment
Edge functions access environment variables at runtime

related.note: Variables are injected at build time and affect deployment behavior
